question: Burt has b action figures, and Cho has c action figures. If Burt gives 4 action figures to Cho, Burt will have twice as many action figures as Cho. Which equation shows the relationship between b and c?

solution.strategy: When you are given a lot of general information about variables, start by writing down what you know. Divide the information into before and after Burt gives 4 of his action figures to Cho.
steps: Before: Burt = b and Cho = c
After: Burt = b - 4 and Cho = c + 4
After: Burt = twice Cho, so b - 4 = 2(c + 4)

question: Burt has b action figures, and Cho has c action figures. If Burt gives 6 action figures to Cho, Burt will have twice as many action figures as Cho. Which equation shows the relationship between b and c?

solution.strategy: Express after-change amounts: Burt = b - 6, Cho = c + 6. Burt has twice as many as Cho: b - 6 = 2(c + 6).
steps: After: Burt = b - 6 and Cho = c + 6
Burt = 2 × Cho gives b - 6 = 2(c + 6)

question: Burt has b action figures, and Cho has c action figures. If Burt gives 3 action figures to Cho, Burt will have three times as many action figures as Cho. Which equation shows the relationship between b and c?

solution.strategy: Express after-change amounts: Burt = b - 3, Cho = c + 3. Burt has three times as many: b - 3 = 3(c + 3).
steps: After: Burt = b - 3 and Cho = c + 3
Burt = 3 × Cho gives b - 3 = 3(c + 3)

question: Burt has b action figures, and Cho has c action figures. If Burt gives 5 action figures to Cho, Cho will have twice as many action figures as Burt. Which equation shows the relationship between b and c?

solution.strategy: After: Burt = b - 5, Cho = c + 5. Cho has twice as many as Burt: c + 5 = 2(b - 5).
steps: After: Burt = b - 5 and Cho = c + 5
Cho = 2 × Burt gives c + 5 = 2(b - 5)

question: Burt has b action figures, and Cho has c action figures. If Burt gives 2 action figures to Cho, Burt will have four times as many action figures as Cho. Which equation shows the relationship between b and c?

solution.strategy: After: Burt = b - 2, Cho = c + 2. Burt has four times as many: b - 2 = 4(c + 2).
steps: After: Burt = b - 2 and Cho = c + 2
Burt = 4 × Cho gives b - 2 = 4(c + 2)

question: Burt has b action figures, and Cho has c action figures. If Burt gives 8 action figures to Cho, Burt will have twice as many action figures as Cho. Which equation shows the relationship between b and c?

solution.strategy: After: Burt = b - 8, Cho = c + 8. Burt = 2 × Cho gives b - 8 = 2(c + 8).
steps: After: Burt = b - 8 and Cho = c + 8

question: Burt has b action figures, and Cho has c action figures. If Cho gives 4 action figures to Burt, Burt will have twice as many action figures as Cho. Which equation shows the relationship between b and c?

solution.strategy: After Cho gives 4 to Burt: Burt = b + 4, Cho = c - 4. Burt = 2 × Cho gives b + 4 = 2(c - 4).
steps: After: Burt = b + 4 and Cho = c - 4
Burt = 2 × Cho gives b + 4 = 2(c - 4)

question: Burt has b action figures, and Cho has c action figures. If Burt gives 7 action figures to Cho, Burt will have the same number of action figures as Cho. Which equation shows the relationship between b and c?

solution.strategy: After: Burt = b - 7, Cho = c + 7. They have the same: b - 7 = c + 7.
steps: After: Burt = b - 7 and Cho = c + 7
Same number: b - 7 = c + 7

question: Burt has b action figures, and Cho has c action figures. If Burt gives 4 action figures to Cho, Cho will have three times as many action figures as Burt. Which equation shows the relationship between b and c?

solution.strategy: After: Burt = b - 4, Cho = c + 4. Cho = 3 × Burt gives c + 4 = 3(b - 4).
steps: After: Burt = b - 4 and Cho = c + 4
Cho = 3 × Burt gives c + 4 = 3(b - 4)

question: Burt has b action figures, and Cho has c action figures. If Burt gives 10 action figures to Cho, Burt will have twice as many action figures as Cho. Which equation shows the relationship between b and c?

solution.strategy: After: Burt = b - 10, Cho = c + 10. Burt = 2 × Cho gives b - 10 = 2(c + 10).
steps: After: Burt = b - 10 and Cho = c + 10
